terms.definition: A field of AI in which systems improve task performance by learning patterns from data—fitting parameters from examples rather than encoding every rule by hand.
The defining ML mechanism: behavior is induced from datasets (experience) through a learning algorithm that adjusts model parameters.
The phase where a model's parameters are fit to training data by minimizing loss or maximizing likelihood.
Applying a trained model to new inputs to produce predictions or actions without further parameter updates.
A model's ability to perform well on unseen data drawn from the same problem, not merely memorize training examples.
Measurable attributes of each example (columns, pixels, tokens) used as input to a model.
Supervised learning targets attached to training examples (class, price, rating) that the model learns to predict.
